page: The Vultr vx1-m-4c-32g-240s is an x86_64 server instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor operating at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided by a 240 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU is present on this instance.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities alongside average multi-core and memory performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is rated as strong at 3056.38, and single-threaded gzip compression achieves a strong 33.72 GB/sec. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches top-tier performance at 14.95 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak at 29.72 GB/sec, and the multi-core stress-ng CPU performance is average at 6112.06. Memory bandwidth metrics are consistently average, with large-block read-only bandwidth at 70.47 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 41.18 GB/sec.

Due to the shared CPU allocation, this instance is best suited for workloads that require substantial memory capacity but do not demand sustained, non-shared multi-core compute power. It is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, static web serving, and lightweight single-threaded processing tasks. The high memory-to-core ratio provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that can tolerate shared CPU resources.
description: The Vultr vx1-m-4c-32g-240s is an x86_64 server instance from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 4 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of memory, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and 240 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmarks demonstrate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 3056.38 and single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching a top-tier 14.95 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded performance is more limited, as shown by an average multi-core stress-ng score of 6112.06 and a weak multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 29.72 GB/sec. This memory-optimized profile, combined with shared CPU allocation, offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and static web serving that do not require dedicated, continuous multi-core processing power.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32g-240s is a memory-optimized server with 4 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 240 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32g-240s is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 240 GB NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive and database workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
240 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
